steps.title: Locate the maximum input
steps.content: Both functions use base $0.3$, which is between $0$ and $1$. So each function decreases as $x$ increases. For $x\geq 0$, the maximum occurs at $x=0$.

steps.title: Check the first equation
steps.content:
For the first equation, substitute $x=0$ to find the maximum value.
$$f(0)=35(0.3)^{0+3}$$
$$f(0)=35(0.3)^3=0.945$$
The maximum value is $0.945$, which is not displayed as a constant or coefficient in the original equation.

steps.title: Check the second equation
steps.content:
For the second equation, use $0.09=(0.3)^2$ and simplify the expression.
$$g(x)=35(0.09)(0.3)^{x-2}$$
$$g(x)=35(0.3)^2(0.3)^{x-2}$$
$$g(x)=35(0.3)^x$$
So $g(0)=35$, and the maximum value is displayed as the coefficient. The answer is B.
